title: Asterisk AMI SIP Digest Authentication Failure Burst
app: Asterisk AMI TCP logger (custom scripted input or `hep`/`syslog` bridge), Splunk Universal Forwarder
spl:
index=voip sourcetype=asterisk:ami (Event="ChallengeResponseFailed" OR Event="InvalidPassword" OR Message="*Authentication*failed*")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count by _time, host, Peer, Account
| where count > 20
description: Brute-force attempts against Asterisk SIP digest authentication or AMI logins often precede toll fraud. AMI emits structured `Event` types for failed challenges; rolling 5-minute counts per peer and account expose automated attacks without needing full PCAP.
value: Reduces toll fraud and trunk denial-of-service by surfacing authentication storms while they are still noisy and blockable at the SBC or firewall.
implementation: Enable AMI read-only for monitoring if possible. Stream AMI over TCP to a lightweight forwarder script that prefixes syslog or writes to a monitored file with `sourcetype=asterisk:ami`. Ensure `Peer`, `Account`, and `Event` fields are extracted. Baseline legitimate password churn; tune `count > 20` per environment. Pair with firewall blocklist automation via SOAR.
visualization: Column chart (failures per 5m), Table (top Peer), Bubble (Account × Peer).
knownFalsePositives: Short bursts of digest failures when phones mass re-register after Wi-Fi/VPN flips, password-expiry days, SBC or certificate work the voice security team posted.

references.title: Splunk Docs: Get data into Splunk from TCP/UDP ports

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• AMI user dedicated to monitoring with `read` only and IP ACL.
• TLS or VPN if AMI crosses a network segment.

Step 1 — Validate field extractions on a known failed login.

Step 2 — Suppress alerts from trusted scanner subnets via lookup.

Step 3 — Correlate with `sourcetype=asterisk:security` if enabled.

Step 4 — Run tabletop with fraud team on auto-block SOP.
grandmaExplanation: We watch SIP auth failures on voice platforms. We help you catch brute-force or config drift before phones stop registering.
telcoUseCase: IMS / enterprise SIP trunk hardening — detect credential stuffing against Asterisk Manager Interface (AMI) or SIP peers before trunks lock or fraud routes appear.
